Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to compute total resolution time for each case Typically total resolution time is from the first event Receive Ticket to the last event Close Ticket The prompt says the first event marks when the ticket was received and the last event marks the closure of the ticket So we will compute from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process flow is Receive Triage Assign Investigate Resolve Close And sometimes there is an escalation to Level 2 Agent which likely happens at different points

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to compute total time from first event to last event for each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Total time from 0800 to 1015 on same day equals 2 hours 15 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Total time from 0805 on March 1 to 0915 on March 2 equals 25 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Total time from 0810 to 0930 on same day equals 1 hour 20 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Total time from 0820 on March 1 to 0830 on March 2 equals 24 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Clearly cases 102 104 and 105 are much longer than 101 and 103

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 102 has long delays between assign and escalate between escalate and investigate and between investigate and resolve

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 has a very long delay between escalate and investigate and also a long delay between investigate and resolve

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Common patterns in long cases are the presence of escalations in cases 102 and 105 and long waiting times between activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In all long cases there is a long gap between investigation and resolution of about 19 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That suggests that after investigation the agent might need to wait until next day to resolve possibly because of approval scheduling or end of day timing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In the short cases investigation happens in the morning and resolution follows quickly while in the long cases investigation happens in the afternoon and resolution occurs the next morning

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalation likely introduces handoff delays and longer processing times due to specialized team workload or prioritization

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Even without escalation case 104 shows that delayed start of investigation after assignment may indicate agent workload issues or weak prioritization

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The process may have a bottleneck at the resolution step after investigation or a queue that causes tickets to wait until the next day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The most significant pattern is that investigation to resolution takes about 19 hours in the long cases but less than 1 hour in the short cases

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The likely root causes are escalation handoff delays long waiting times before investigation and delays between investigation and resolution

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations include reducing escalation rates improving assignment efficiency streamlining the post investigation process addressing Level 2 delays and investigating why work starts late in the day
